step_by_step_thinking:
I concur with the earlier conclusion that thymocytes do not fall under the category of antigen-presenting cells due to their primary role in developing into functional T-cells during immune system maturation. Unlike other cell types listed such as macrophages which actively take up, process, and present foreign substances to activate adaptive immunity, thymocytes' main function lies elsewhere without engaging extensively in direct antigen presentation. 

Answer: D``` markdown. .
